notes: Helper correctness is locked down programmatically by test_render_markmap.py (run: python evals/test_render_markmap.py). The prompts below are realistic invocation cases for trigger/behavior review.

evals.prompt:
Turn this outline into an interactive mindmap I can click through and search:

# Compliance Program
## Policies
- Code of conduct
- Anti-bribery
## Training
- Onboarding
- Annual refresher
evals.expected_output: A standalone HTML mind map (dark background, white font) rendered with markmap.js; nodes match the headings/bullets; search filters the tree to matches + ancestors + descendants.

evals.prompt: Embed a markmap of my project plan in my Streamlit app, starting collapsed to the top two levels with an expand-all toggle.
evals.expected_output: Uses render_markmap() inside Streamlit via components.html; initialExpandLevel set to 2 by default; an expand-all path sets level -1. Dark iframe background so the white font is readable.

evals.prompt: My mind map shows up blank/empty when I open the HTML file in the browser — the text seems invisible. Fix it.
evals.expected_output: Diagnoses white-font-on-white/transparent-background; build_html paints its own dark backdrop (#0e1117) by default so the white font is visible standalone.

evals.prompt: Make a mindmap from these notes that include some code generics like List and a step that says 'collapse with '.
evals.expected_output: Map renders fully without truncation; '<', '>', '&' in node text are HTML-escaped by build_html and round-trip to markmap unchanged (no early break, no dropped ).
